verses.latin_vulgate: Sapientia ædificavit sibi domum :  excidit columnas septem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חׇ֭כְמוֹת בָּנְתָ֣ה בֵיתָ֑הּ חָצְבָ֖ה עַמּוּדֶ֣יהָ שִׁבְעָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Wisdom hath built herself a house, she hath hewn her out seven pillars.
verses.finnish_biblia: Viisaus rakensi huoneensa, ja vuoli siihen seitsemän patsasta,
verses.king_james: Wisdom hath builded her house, she hath hewn out her seven pillars:

verses.latin_vulgate: Immolavit victimas suas, miscuit vinum,  et proposuit mensam suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טָבְחָ֣ה טִ֭בְחָהּ מָסְכָ֣ה יֵינָ֑הּ אַ֝֗ף עָרְכָ֥ה שֻׁלְחָנָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: She hath slain her victims, mingled her wine, and set forth her table.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja teurasti teuraansa, sekoitti viinansa ja valmisti pöytänsä,
verses.king_james: She hath killed her beasts; she hath mingled her wine; she hath also furnished her table.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit ancillas suas ut vocarent  ad arcem et ad mœnia civitatis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁלְחָ֣ה נַעֲרֹתֶ֣יהָ תִקְרָ֑א עַל־גַּ֝פֵּ֗י מְרֹ֣מֵי קָֽרֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: She hath sent her maids to invite to the tower, and to the walls of the city:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja lähetti piikansa, korkeista kaupungin saleista kutsumaan:
verses.king_james: She hath sent forth her maidens: she crieth upon the highest places of the city,

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis est parvulus, veniat ad me.  Et insipientibus locuta est :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִֽי־פֶ֭תִי יָסֻ֣ר הֵ֑נָּה חֲסַר־לֵ֝֗ב אָ֣מְרָה לּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Whosoever is a little one, let him come to me. And to the unwise she said:
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka tyhmä on, hän tulkaan tänne; ja hulluille sanoi hän:
verses.king_james: Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither: as for him that wanteth understanding, she saith to him,

verses.latin_vulgate: Venite, comedite panem meum,  et bibite vinum quod miscui vobis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְ֭כוּ לַחֲמ֣וּ בְֽלַחֲמִ֑י וּ֝שְׁת֗וּ בְּיַ֣יִן מָסָֽכְתִּי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Come, eat my bread, and drink the wine which I have mingled for you.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tulkaat ja syökäät minun leivästäni, ja juokaat viinaa, jonka minä sekoitin.
verses.king_james: Come, eat of my bread, and drink of the wine which I have mingled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Relinquite infantiam, et vivite,  et ambulate per vias prudentiæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עִזְב֣וּ פְתָאיִ֣ם וִֽחְי֑וּ וְ֝אִשְׁר֗וּ בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ בִּינָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Forsake childishness, and live, and walk by the ways of prudence.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hyljätkää hullu meno, niin te saatte elää, ja käykää ymmärryksen tiellä.
verses.king_james: Forsake the foolish, and live; and go in the way of understanding.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui erudit derisorem, ipse injuriam sibi facit,  et qui arguit impium, sibi maculam generat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֹ֤סֵ֨ר ׀ לֵ֗ץ לֹקֵ֣חַֽ ל֣וֹ קָל֑וֹן וּמוֹכִ֖יחַ לְרָשָׁ֣ע מוּמֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that teacheth a scorner, doth an injury to himself; and he that rebuketh a wicked man, getteth himself a blot.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka pilkkaajaa kurittaa, hän saa häpiän, ja joka jumalatointa nuhtelee, hän häväistään.
verses.king_james: He that reproveth a scorner getteth to himself shame: and he that rebuketh a wicked man getteth himself a blot.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noli arguere derisorem, ne oderit te :  argue sapientem, et diliget te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תּ֣וֹכַח לֵ֭ץ פֶּן־יִשְׂנָאֶ֑ךָּ הוֹכַ֥ח לְ֝חָכָ֗ם וְיֶאֱהָבֶֽךָּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Rebuke not a scorner, lest he hate thee. Rebuke a wise man, and he will love thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä rankaise pilkkaajaa, ettei hän sinua vihaisi: nuhtele viisasta, ja hän rakastaa sinua.
verses.king_james: Reprove not a scorner, lest he hate thee: rebuke a wise man, and he will love thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Da sapienti occasionem, et addetur ei sapientia ;  doce justum, et festinabit accipere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תֵּ֣ן לְ֭חָכָם וְיֶחְכַּם־ע֑וֹד הוֹדַ֥ע לְ֝צַדִּ֗יק וְי֣וֹסֶף לֶֽקַח׃
verses.douay_rheims: Give an occasion to a wise man, and wisdom shall be added to him. Teach a just man, and he shall make haste to receive it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Anna viisaalle, niin hän viisaammaksi tulee: opeta vanhurskasta, niin hän opissaan etenee.
verses.king_james: Give instruction to a wise man, and he will be yet wiser: teach a just man, and he will increase in learning.

verses.latin_vulgate: Principium sapientiæ timor Domini,  et scientia sanctorum prudentia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּחִלַּ֣ת חׇ֭כְמָה יִרְאַ֣ת יְהֹוָ֑ה וְדַ֖עַת קְדֹשִׁ֣ים בִּינָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy is prudence.
verses.finnish_biblia: Viisauden alku on Herran pelko, ja pyhäin tieto on ymmärrys.
verses.king_james: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy is understanding.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per me enim multiplicabuntur dies tui,  et addentur tibi anni vitæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־בִ֭י יִרְבּ֣וּ יָמֶ֑יךָ וְיוֹסִ֥יפוּ לְּ֝ךָ֗ שְׁנ֣וֹת חַיִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: For by me shall thy days be multiplied, and years of life shall be added to thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä minun kauttani sinun päiväs enennetään, ja ikäs vuodet lisätään.
verses.king_james: For by me thy days shall be multiplied, and the years of thy life shall be increased.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si sapiens fueris, tibimetipsi eris ;  si autem illusor, solus portabis malum.
verses.douay_rheims: If thou be wise, thou shalt be so to thyself: and if a scorner, thou alone shalt bear the evil.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos sinä olet viisas, niin sinä itselles viisas olet; mutta jos sinä olet pilkkaaja, niin sinä itse sen kannat.
verses.king_james: If thou be wise, thou shalt be wise for thyself: but if thou scornest, thou alone shalt bear it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mulier stulta et clamosa,  plenaque illecebris, et nihil omnino sciens,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֣שֶׁת כְּ֭סִילוּת הֹמִיָּ֑ה פְּ֝תַיּ֗וּת וּבַל־יָ֥דְעָה מָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: A foolish woman and clamorous, and full of allurements, and knowing nothing at all,
verses.finnish_biblia: Hullu, huikentelevainen vaimo, mieletöin ei tiedä mitään,
verses.king_james: A foolish woman is clamorous: she is simple, and knoweth nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: sedit in foribus domus suæ,  super sellam in excelso urbis loco,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽ֭יָשְׁבָה לְפֶ֣תַח בֵּיתָ֑הּ עַל־כִּ֝סֵּ֗א מְרֹ֣מֵי קָֽרֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Sat at the door of her house, upon a seat, in a high place of the city,
verses.finnish_biblia: Istuu huoneensa ovella, korkialla istuimella, ylimmäisessä siassa kaupungissa,
verses.king_james: For she sitteth at the door of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city,

verses.latin_vulgate: ut vocaret transeuntes per viam,  et pergentes itinere suo :
verses.douay_rheims: To call them that pass by the way, and go on their journey:
verses.finnish_biblia: Että hän houkuttelis kaikkia, jotka siitä käyvät ohitse, ja tietänsä vaeltavat:
verses.king_james: To call passengers who go right on their ways:

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui est parvulus declinet ad me.  Et vecordi locuta est :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִֽי־פֶ֭תִי יָסֻ֣ר הֵ֑נָּה וַחֲסַר־לֵ֝֗ב וְאָ֣מְרָה לּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that is a little one, let him turn to me. And to the fool she said:
verses.finnish_biblia: Se joka tyhmä on, hän tulkoon tänne; ja hullulle sanoo hän:
verses.king_james: Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither: and as for him that wanteth understanding, she saith to him,

verses.latin_vulgate: Aquæ furtivæ dulciores sunt,  et panis absconditus suavior.
verses.douay_rheims: Stolen waters are sweeter, and hidden bread is more pleasant.
verses.finnish_biblia: Varastettu vesi on makia, ja salattu leipä suloinen;
verses.king_james: Stolen waters are sweet, and bread eaten in secret is pleasant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ignoravit quod ibi sint gigantes,  et in profundis inferni convivæ ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽלֹא־יָ֭דַע כִּֽי־רְפָאִ֣ים שָׁ֑ם בְּעִמְקֵ֖י שְׁא֣וֹל קְרֻאֶֽיהָ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And he did not know that giants are there, and that her guests are in the depths of hell.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan ei hän tiedä, että siellä kuolleet ovat: hänen vieraansa ovat helvetin syvyydessä.
verses.king_james: But he knoweth not that the dead are there; and that her guests are in the depths of hell.
